steps.title: Calculate sample proportion and scale to population
steps.content:
The sample data shows 20 out of 80 customers purchased fruit. This gives a sample proportion of:
$$\frac{20}{80} = \frac{1}{4} = 0.25$$
To estimate how many of the total 1,500 customers purchased fruit, apply this proportion to the full population:
$$1,500 \times \frac{1}{4} = 375 \text{ customers}$$

steps.title: Identify appropriate language and select the choice
steps.content:
When inferring population characteristics from a sample, the correct statistical language is best estimate, not exactly must have. Sample-based conclusions always carry uncertainty because we observed only a portion of the population.

This eliminates choices A and B—both incorrectly claim the count must be exact. Between the remaining choices:
  • Choice C: Estimates 75 (incorrect count)
  • Choice D: Estimates 375 (matches our calculation) with appropriate statistical language
That matches choice D — D is correct.
